title: Floral Crunchy Spanish Squid Soup
description: A light, aromatic soup featuring tender baby squid, bright citrus, and a floral garnish, finished with a crunchy pine nut topping for added texture and energy.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a medium pot over medium heat.
Add the diced onion, carrot, and celery; sauté until the vegetables soften, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, tomato paste, smoked paprika, and cumin; cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Deglaze the pot with a splash of stock, scraping up any browned bits.
Add the remaining stock, saffron threads, and bring the mixture to a gentle boil.
Reduce heat to low and add the halved baby squid; simmer for 8-10 minutes until the squid is opaque and tender.
Stir in the lemon juice, salt, and pepper; adjust seasoning as needed.
Transfer the soup to serving bowls, ensuring each has a generous portion of squid.
Garnish with toasted pine nuts and a scattering of edible marigold petals for a floral, crunchy finish.
Serve immediately while hot, optionally with a side of crisp whole30-friendly bread.
